bullet_points: Load the hayashi_grid.json and hayashi_grid_summary.csv datasets.
Compute the fractional change in N_abs across the f_cool grid at fixed M_cut to verify the thermal saturation regime.
Print a detailed numerical table of N_abs values across all (z, f_cool, M_cut) combinations, including CDM baseline values and fractional suppressions.
Save the summary statistics and verification metrics to a CSV file for subsequent steps.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Baseline Calibration and Thermal Saturation Verification

bullet_points: Generate a two-panel plot of N(>tau) curves: one panel for varying M_cut at fixed z and f_cool=1.0, and another for varying f_cool at fixed z and M_cut=0.
Visualize how structure suppression and cooling modify the cumulative absorber count.
Save the plots as high-resolution PNG files.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Visualization of the 21 cm Forest Signal

bullet_points: Compute the fractional change Delta N_abs/N_abs as a function of M_cut and f_cool for each redshift.

Define a physically motivated parametric model for nuisance parameters J_21 and x_HI_bar, and compute approximate analytic partial derivatives of the absorber distribution with respect to these parameters.
Quantify the degradation of the M_cut constraint due to these astrophysical degeneracies.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Sensitivity Quantification and Astrophysical Nuisance Modeling

bullet_points: Construct a Fisher matrix for {M_cut, J_21, x_HI_bar} for each redshift slice, assuming N_sightlines = 10 and specified fiducial values/prior ranges for nuisance parameters.
Incorporate a visibility factor N_eff(z) to account for reionization patchiness and Poisson noise.
Compute the Fisher information content per tau-bin and derive an optimal weighting scheme for tau ranges to maximize the signal-to-noise ratio for M_cut.
Plot the marginalized uncertainty sigma(M_cut) as a function of z to identify the optimal observational window.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Redshift-Dependent Fisher Forecast and Optimal Window Identification

bullet_points: Map the marginalized 1-sigma and 2-sigma confidence intervals of M_cut to the DM-baryon scattering cross-section sigma_0/m_chi for n=0 and n=-4 models.
Generate exclusion contour plots in the sigma_0/m_chi vs. m_chi plane, overplotting existing literature constraints from CMB and global 21 cm experiments as shaded regions.
Interpret the quantitative results from the Fisher analysis and sensitivity mapping.
Quantitatively compare the structure suppression channel sensitivity versus the thermal channel sensitivity using the ratio of Fisher information and the ratio of fractional N_abs changes.
Discuss the unique complementarity of the 21 cm forest in probing the structure suppression channel.
Write the full Results section of the paper, incorporating all quantitative findings, interpretations of the plots, and key statistics.
Explicitly reference each figure by content description (e.g., "the N(>tau) suppression figure," "the sigma(M_cut) vs. z figure," "the exclusion contour figure").
Include all key numerical results, such as the optimal redshift, marginalized sigma(M_cut) values, and corresponding cross-section sensitivities, ensuring the Results section is self-contained.
